prompt:
Apply the working push notification configuration changes that were tested locally.

## Context
We just finished setting up the GitHub webhook → github-relay → Klaus push notification pipeline. These changes were tested and confirmed working on the live system. They need to be committed as a PR.

## Changes needed

### 1. home/dev.nix — Add webhook config to klaus config block
In the `home.file.".klaus/config.json".text` section, add a `webhook` block after `auto_merge_on_approval = true;`:

```nix
      webhook = {
        port = 9800;
        path = "/webhook/github";
        poll_fallback = false;
      };
```

### 2. hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix — Fix consumer URL and secret permissions

a) Change the comment above the klaus consumer from:
   `# Forward PR/CI events to klaus microVM (10.100.0.2 on br-klaus bridge)`
   to:
   `# Forward PR/CI events to klaus`

b) Change the consumer URL from:
   `url = "http://10.100.0.2:9800/webhook/github";`
   to:
   `url = "http://127.0.0.1:9800/webhook/github";`

c) Change the github-webhook-secret mode from:
   `mode = "0400";`
   to:
   `mode = "0444";`

### 3. secrets/github-webhook-secret.age — Populate the encrypted secret
Replace the empty file with the base64-decoded content below:

```
 veQHee/xRwqmO0wClqjHNJGW3DWOCi6Xl65saC1rbnDG/ToCR0o6tyhcMdmBYIicougAtYsJBXRUDKv2Vb1Kr41UzD4ao70hkVv406m+qbX8wUmuvhnhsZz7wtAYWYWMeQ==
```

To write this file: `echo '<base64>' | base64 -d > secrets/github-webhook-secret.age`

### 4. flake.lock — Update flake inputs
Run `nix flake lock --update-input github-relay --update-input klaus --update-input nixpkgs` to pick up the latest revisions of these inputs.

## PR details
- Title: feat: configure webhook push notifications for klaus pipeline
- The PR should explain that this switches the klaus pipeline from 30s polling to real-time push notifications via github-relay, and fixes the consumer URL and secret permissions that were needed to get it working.

## Constraints
- Do NOT run nixos-rebuild or any deployment commands
- The flake lock update needs nix available — run `which nix` first and if unavailable just skip that step and note it in the PR
